name: bats-testing-patterns
description: Master Bash Automated Testing System (Bats) for comprehensive shell script testing. Use when writing tests for shell scripts, CI/CD pipelines, or requiring test-driven development of shell utilities.
capabilities: Write unit tests for shell scripts using BATS framework
Create test fixtures and helper libraries for complex test scenarios
Implement setup and teardown patterns for test isolation
Mock and stub external commands and functions for unit testing
Test error conditions and edge cases in shell scripts
Integrate BATS tests into CI/CD workflows
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill provides documentation and examples for writing BATS tests. All 91 static findings are false positives: external commands are code examples in markdown, network URLs are documentation links, filesystem patterns are legitimate test setup patterns, and weak crypto detection is a false positive due to 'Bats' being misidentified as a cryptographic reference.
valueStatement: Learn comprehensive BATS testing patterns to write maintainable, reliable tests for shell scripts in CI/CD pipelines.
userTitle: Write Robust Shell Script Tests with BATS
limitations: Only tests shell script behavior, not other programming languages
Does not provide integration testing beyond shell execution
Requires BATS to be installed on the test environment
Shell compatibility varies across different shells (bash, sh, dash)

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Testing Infrastructure Scripts
useCases.description: Write automated tests for deployment scripts, configuration management scripts, and infrastructure automation to ensure reliability.

useCases.title: Developer Adding Tests to Shell Utilities
useCases.description: Implement test-driven development for CLI tools and shell utilities to catch regressions early.

useCases.title: Team Establishing Shell Script Quality Standards
useCases.description: Set up comprehensive testing patterns and best practices across a team to maintain consistent code quality.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Test Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a BATS test file for my shell script at script.sh that tests the exit code and output of the main function.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to add your first test to a shell script

promptTemplates.title: Test with Fixtures
promptTemplates.prompt: Write BATS tests that use fixture files from tests/fixtures/ directory to test file processing functions.
promptTemplates.scenario: When testing functions that read or write files

promptTemplates.title: Mocking External Commands
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to stub the 'curl' command in BATS to test an API function without making real network requests.
promptTemplates.scenario: When testing scripts that depend on external commands

promptTemplates.title: CI/CD Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow and Makefile to run BATS tests in CI/CD with TAP output.
promptTemplates.scenario: When setting up automated test execution

bestPractices: Use descriptive test names that clearly state what is being tested and expected behavior
Always clean up temporary files and resources in teardown functions to prevent test pollution
Mock external dependencies like API calls and file system operations to isolate unit tests
antiPatterns: Testing multiple unrelated assertions in a single test function violates single responsibility
Skipping teardown or cleanup leads to flaky tests and resource leaks
Testing implementation details rather than observable behavior makes tests brittle

faq.question: What is BATS?
faq.answer: BATS (Bash Automated Testing System) is a testing framework for shell scripts that provides TAP-compliant output and simple test syntax.

faq.question: How do I install BATS?
faq.answer: Install via Homebrew (brew install bats-core), npm (npm install --global bats), or clone from GitHub and run the install script.

faq.question: What is the difference between setup and setup_file?
faq.answer: setup() runs before each test, while setup_file() runs once before all tests in a file. Use setup_file for expensive shared resources.

faq.question: How do I test that a command fails?
faq.answer: Use 'run' to execute the command, then assert that $status is non-zero and optionally check the error output.

faq.question: Can I test scripts that use external commands?
faq.answer: Yes, use function mocking with 'export -f' or command stubbing by creating mock executables in a temporary PATH directory.

faq.question: How do I run tests in parallel?
faq.answer: Use the --parallel flag with bats, for example: bats tests/*.bats --parallel 4
